ddls:
CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "app_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the project the application belongs to */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the team responsible for the application */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the application is deployed (e.g., development, staging, production) */,
  "current_version" TEXT /* Current version of the application */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the application */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the application was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the application was last updated */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the application */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the application */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("app_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the project (e.g., active, inactive, completed) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the project */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the project the team belongs to */,
  "lead_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team lead */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the team */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the team */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the team */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "application_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the application */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  "changelog" TEXT /* Changelog for the version */,
  "changelog_url" TEXT /* URL of the changelog for the version */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the version is the current version */,
  "released_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who released the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_application_versions_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the version of the application */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the metric */,
  "running_instances" INTEGER /* Number of running instances of the application */,
  "percent_covered" REAL /* Percentage of code coverage */,
  "total_lines" INTEGER /* Total number of lines of code */,
  "covered_lines" INTEGER /* Number of covered lines of code */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., running instances, percent covered) */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric (e.g., count, percentage) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES application_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, operations, admin) */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the team the user belongs to */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application being accessed */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the version of the application being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_type_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the access type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES application_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance report */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the version of the application */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV) */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reports_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES application_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reports_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_anomalies" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance anomaly */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the version of the application */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the anomaly */,
  "anomaly_type" TEXT /* Type of the anomaly (e.g., high CPU usage, low memory) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the anomaly */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the anomaly has been resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the anomaly */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_anomalies_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_anomalies_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES application_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance trend */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the version of the application */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the trend */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the trend */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of the metric (e.g., running_instances, percent_covered) */,
  "trend_description" TEXT /* Description of the trend */,
  "trend_type" TEXT /* Type of the trend (e.g., increasing, decreasing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_trends_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES application_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_trends_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the role (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the role */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
